title: OJCP Verifier Manifest
description: Schema for the /.well-known/ojcp-verifier.json file hosted by identity verification providers.

description.description: Brief description of the verifier's services.

verification_types.description: Types of verification this provider supports.

verification_endpoint.description: URL for initiating verification sessions. Providers POST a session request to this endpoint to create a scoped verification_url for the candidate.

proof_delivery_methods.description: Methods this verifier supports for delivering proofs. 'callback': verifier POSTs proof to the provider's callback_url (server-to-server). 'redirect': verifier redirects candidate's browser to redirect_url with proof_token as a query parameter. 'polling': agent or provider polls the verifier's session status endpoint to retrieve the proof.

proof_format.description: Format of the verification proof artifact. 'jws' for signed proofs per RFC 7515. 'jws+jwe' signals that the verifier supports signed-then-encrypted proofs per RFC 7516, but JWE handling rules are not defined in OJCP v0.1 — providers SHOULD reject jws+jwe proofs they cannot decrypt. Full JWE support will be defined in a future version.

signing_algorithms.description: JWS signing algorithms supported by this verifier. ES256 is RECOMMENDED.

proof_ttl_seconds.description: Default time-to-live for issued proofs, in seconds.

public_keys_url.description: URL to a JWK Set containing the verifier's public keys for proof validation.

supported_countries.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country codes where this verifier operates.
